---
pagetitle: "EpiAware — composable tools for infectious disease modelling"
description: "A composable Julia ecosystem for infectious disease modelling."
toc: false
page-layout: full
listing:
  - id: news-listing
    contents: news/posts
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    max-items: 3
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title, description]
---

::: {.hero}
![](assets/img/logo.svg){.hero-logo fig-alt="EpiAware logo"}

# EpiAware

<p class="lead">Composable tools for infectious disease modelling in Julia.</p>

::: {.cta-row}
[Explore the packages](packages/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg role="button"}
[Get involved](get-involved.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"}
[GitHub](https://github.com/EpiAware){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-lg role="button"}
:::
:::

::: {.landing-split}
::: {.landing-points}
- **Composable** — small parts that combine into joint models.
- **Uncertainty-aware** — uncertainty propagated correctly across components.
- **Built for response** — designed for real-time outbreak analysis.
- **Built on Julia** — fast, autodiff-ready, on top of Turing and SciML.

[Get started →](quickstart.qmd){.more-link}
:::

::: {.landing-code}
[Compose distributions]{.code-label}

```julia
using ComposedDistributions, ModifiedDistributions, Distributions

# Compose a case's delays; affine() rescales one of them
tree = Sequential([
    Gamma(2.0, 1.5),
    compose((admission = affine(LogNormal(1.0, 0.5); scale = 1.5),
             death     = Gamma(2.0, 3.0))),
])
```

[Assemble and fit a model]{.code-label}

```julia
using ComposableTuringIDModels, Distributions

# A random-walk latent process driving direct infections,
# observed with Poisson error
model = IDModel(
    DirectInfections(; Z = RandomWalk(),
        initialisation_prior = Normal()),
    PoissonError())

# Turn it into a Turing model and fit it to case data
chain = sample(as_turing_model(model, cases, length(cases)),
               NUTS(), MCMCThreads(), 1_000, 2)
```
:::
:::

## Core packages

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### [ComposableTuringIDModels.jl](packages/composableturingidmodels.qmd)
Assemble infectious disease models from interchangeable parts as a single
Turing model. [Docs ↗](https://composableturingidmodels.epiaware.org/dev/)
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [CensoredDistributions.jl](packages/censoreddistributions.qmd)
Handle the censoring and truncation biases common in epidemiological delay
data. [Docs ↗](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/)
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [ComposedDistributions.jl](packages/composeddistributions.qmd)
A grammar for composing distributions into event trees.
[Docs ↗](https://composeddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/)
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [ModifiedDistributions.jl](packages/modifieddistributions.qmd)
Rescale, weight, or modify distributions, keeping the full interface.
[Docs ↗](https://modifieddistributions.epiaware.org/dev/)
:::
:::

[See all packages →](packages/index.qmd){.more-link}

## From the blog

::: {#news-listing}
:::

[All news →](news/index.qmd){.more-link}

## Built on Julia

We build in Julia for its type system, multiple dispatch, and automatic
differentiation, and on the scientific infrastructure already there:

::: {.feature-grid .foundations}
::: {.feature-card}
### [SciML](https://sciml.ai)
Differential equations, sensitivity analysis, and scientific machine learning.
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [Turing.jl](https://turinglang.org)
Probabilistic programming and Bayesian inference.
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### [Distributions.jl](https://github.com/JuliaStats/Distributions.jl)
The shared vocabulary of probability distributions our packages extend.
:::
:::

New to Julia? Start with the [Using Julia](using-julia.qmd) guide.

## Background

In R we built the [epinowcast](community.qmd) ecosystem and packages such as
[EpiNow2](https://github.com/epiforecasts/EpiNow2) and
[scoringutils](https://github.com/epiforecasts/scoringutils), now used by
academics and public health teams internationally. EpiAware is the Julia
equivalent: a domain-focused ecosystem in the mould of SciML or Turing.jl,
with community infrastructure like [rOpenSci](https://ropensci.org) and the
domain focus of [SpeedyWeather.jl](https://github.com/SpeedyWeather/SpeedyWeather.jl).

We are at an early stage and looking for collaborators —
[here is how to get involved](get-involved.qmd).
